nodes.label: NOTE: This file should not be edited
nodes.norm_label: note: this file should not be edited

nodes.label: Graphify Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Turns any folder of code/docs/papers/images/video into a persistent, queryable knowledge graph with an honest audit trail; community detection surfaces cross-document connections users would not think to ask about.

nodes.label: AST Structural Extraction
nodes.rationale: Deterministic and free: code is extracted via AST with no LLM and no API key, so a code-only corpus skips semantic extraction entirely. Run in parallel with semantic subagents to save 5-15s on large corpora.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction Subagents
nodes.rationale: Docs/papers/images need LLM extraction; parallel general-purpose subagents (chunks of 20-25 files, dispatched in one message) are 5-10x faster than reading files serially. Explore-type agents are forbidden because they are read-only and silently drop chunk files.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction Cache
nodes.rationale: Cache entries are attributed to the extraction prompt file (SPEC_PATH): when a graphify upgrade changes the prompt, old entries are re-extracted instead of replayed; unchanged prompts keep their entries (#1939).

nodes.label: Honesty Rules
nodes.rationale: Never invent an edge (use AMBIGUOUS when unsure), never skip corpus warnings, always show token cost, show raw cohesion scores, warn before visualizing graphs over 5000 nodes.

nodes.label: Graph Health Check
nodes.rationale: Read-only integrity gate before labeling; surfaces edge collapse, dangling/missing endpoints, and self-loops - the silent-corruption modes of incremental updates and AST/LLM id mismatches. Never aborts, but warnings must be surfaced per the Honesty Rules.

nodes.label: Fast Path Query on Existing Graph
nodes.rationale: When graphify-out/graph.json exists and the request is a natural-language question, skip the entire build pipeline and run graphify query immediately - the graph is already built.
nodes.norm_label: fast path query on existing graph

nodes.label: Graph Shrink Guard
nodes.rationale: to_json refuses to write when the new graph is smaller than the existing graph.json (#479), and GRAPH_REPORT.md is only written when the graph was actually written, so reports never describe a graph that graph.json does not contain (#1392).

nodes.label: Gemini Backend Fallback
nodes.rationale: graphify needs no API key and must never block on one: Gemini is used only if GEMINI_API_KEY/GOOGLE_API_KEY is already set; otherwise the host agent itself is the LLM. No other provider keys are read.

nodes.label: URL Ingest (/graphify add)
nodes.rationale: Auto-detects URL type (YouTube via yt-dlp, Twitter via oEmbed, arXiv, PDF, images, webpages via html2text) and saves to ./raw, then runs the --update pipeline to merge the new file into the graph.

nodes.label: Watch Mode Auto-Rebuild
nodes.rationale: Debounce (default 3s) waits until file activity stops so a wave of parallel agent writes does not trigger a rebuild per file. Code-only changes rebuild immediately with no LLM; doc/image changes only flag needs_update.

nodes.label: Wiki Export
nodes.rationale: Must run before Step 9 cleanup so .graphify_labels.json is still available.

nodes.label: Neo4j Export
nodes.rationale: Uses MERGE statements so re-running never creates duplicates.

nodes.label: FalkorDB Export
nodes.rationale: Prefer --falkordb-push over the cypher.txt artifact because FalkorDB's GRAPH.QUERY runs one statement at a time with no bulk script import. The falkordb:// scheme is informational - redis:// or bare host:port also work.

nodes.label: Graphify MCP Server
nodes.rationale: Stdio MCP server exposing query_graph, get_node, get_neighbors, get_community, god_nodes, graph_stats, shortest_path so other agents can query the graph live. Claude Desktop config must use the absolute interpreter path because it cannot run $(...).

nodes.label: Token Reduction Benchmark
nodes.rationale: Only runs when total_words > 5000; for small corpora the graph's value is structural clarity, not token compression.

nodes.label: Extraction Subagent Prompt
nodes.rationale: Loaded only when a chunk holds docs/papers/images; a pure-code corpus never reads it. source_file must be copied verbatim from FILE_LIST so full builds and incremental --update stay on the same base and build_merge replace-on-re-extract matches existing nodes.

nodes.label: Discrete Confidence Rubric
nodes.rationale: Models follow discrete rubrics better than continuous ranges: production showed a bimodal collapse (>50% at 0.5, >40% at 0.85+), so INFERRED scores are restricted to five fixed values (0.95/0.85/0.75/0.65/0.55) and 0.5 is banned.

nodes.label: Full-Path Node ID Format
nodes.rationale: IDs use the full repo-relative path stem so same-named files in different directories stay distinct and semantic IDs match AST-generated IDs; filename-only or parent-only stems create orphan ghost-duplicate nodes. IDs must be deterministic - never append chunk suffixes.

nodes.label: Hyperedges
nodes.rationale: Used sparingly (max 3 per chunk) only when a group relationship among 3+ nodes adds information beyond pairwise edges.

nodes.label: GitHub Repo Clone
nodes.rationale: Clones into ~/.graphify/repos/<owner>/<repo> and reuses existing clones on repeat runs.

nodes.label: Cross-Repo Graph Merge
nodes.rationale: For monorepos/multi-service layouts the skill pipeline would clobber a shared graphify-out/, so each subfolder is extracted via the CLI (which nests graphify-out inside the scanned path) and then merge-graphs combines them; merged nodes carry a repo attribute for filtering by origin.

nodes.label: Post-Commit Auto-Rebuild Hook
nodes.rationale: No background process needed - triggers once per commit, detects changed code files via git diff HEAD~1, re-runs AST extraction only. Doc/image changes are ignored by the hook. Appends to an existing post-commit hook rather than replacing it.

nodes.label: Native CLAUDE.md Integration
nodes.rationale: graphify claude install writes a '## graphify' section to the project CLAUDE.md so Claude checks the graph before answering codebase questions and rebuilds after code changes - no manual /graphify needed in future sessions.

nodes.label: Constrained Query Expansion
nodes.rationale: The query matcher is case-folded substring + IDF with no stemming, synonyms, or cross-language matching, so vocabulary mismatch collapses answers to noise. The fix expands the question against tokens actually present in graph labels - never inventing tokens - and prints the selection so the expansion is auditable.

nodes.label: BFS/DFS Graph Traversal
nodes.rationale: BFS (default) for broad context questions, DFS (depth-limited to 6) for tracing a specific chain; output is token-budget aware, ranked by term relevance and truncated at ~4 chars/token.

nodes.label: Work Memory (save-result Loop)
nodes.rationale: Self-improving loop: every query/path/explain answer is saved back with an outcome (useful/dead_end/corrected), and graphify reflect distills them into LESSONS.md listing preferred sources, known dead ends, and corrections that future sessions read at start.

nodes.label: Whisper Video Transcription
nodes.rationale: The host agent composes a one-sentence domain hint from god-node labels and passes it as Whisper's initial prompt (GRAPHIFY_WHISPER_PROMPT) - no separate API call needed. Transcripts are then treated as doc files for semantic extraction. JSON is written from Python, not a shell redirect, because Whisper prints progress to stdout (#1392).

nodes.label: Incremental Update (--update)
nodes.rationale: Only re-extracts new or changed files to save tokens and time. Code-only change sets skip semantic extraction entirely (no LLM); changed video files must be transcribed first or raw media paths reach subagents as unreadable input (#1392).

nodes.label: build_merge Replace-on-Re-extract
nodes.rationale: Reads graph.json directly without a NetworkX round-trip so edge direction is always preserved (#801). prune_sources is only for genuinely deleted files - changed files are reconciled by replace-on-re-extract (#1344); root= relativizes prune paths so stale nodes do not accumulate (#1361); directed must be threaded through or a --directed --update silently collapses reciprocal edges (#1392).

nodes.label: Cluster-Only Rerun
nodes.rationale: Self-contained: re-clusters, names communities, and regenerates report/graph/HTML from the existing graph. Steps 5-9 must not be re-run because they read intermediate files a prior build's cleanup already deleted (#1392).

nodes.label: Pull Request Template and Checklist
nodes.rationale: Gates merges on pnpm test/lint/typecheck passing, no committed secrets, and PRs scoped to one concern (no mixing features with refactors).

nodes.label: Project CLAUDE.md Graphify Rules
nodes.rationale: Directs codebase questions to graphify query/path/explain first because they return a scoped subgraph much smaller than GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw grep output; graphify update . after code changes is AST-only with no API cost.

nodes.label: Local Development Setup
nodes.rationale: AI uses the local Claude Code CLI so no API key is needed; Postgres and Redis run via Docker Compose; apps serve at web:3000 and api:8000.

nodes.label: Trunk-Based Branch Strategy
nodes.rationale: Trunk-based development with short-lived typed feature branches; main requires PR + 1 approval + passing CI with no direct pushes, and feature branches squash-merge to keep main history clean.

nodes.label: Code Review Process
nodes.rationale: PRs stay under ~400 changed lines and one logical change; reviewers respond within 1 business day and mark comments [BLOCKING]/[OPTIONAL]/[NIT]; the author (not the reviewer) merges after approval.

nodes.label: CI/CD Pipeline
nodes.rationale: push/PR triggers lint -> typecheck -> test -> build, all merge-blocking; main pushes additionally deploy to staging followed by a smoke test.

nodes.label: Semver Release Process
nodes.rationale: Pushing a version tag drives release.yml, which builds, deploys to production, and creates a GitHub Release; hotfixes branch off main, skip develop, and tag a patch immediately after merge.

nodes.label: Testing Standards
nodes.rationale: Vitest for unit/integration, Playwright for E2E; minimum 60% coverage on core modules, new features ship with tests, bug fixes include a regression test.

nodes.label: PostgreSQL + pgvector Service
nodes.rationale: Uses the pgvector/pgvector:pg16 image, implying vector similarity search (embeddings) is part of the local data stack.

nodes.label: pnpm Workspace Monorepo Layout
nodes.rationale: Packages live under apps/* and packages/*, the structure that enables pnpm --filter commands like @sultan-saif/db migrations.

links.source_location: serves the pipeline's graph.json output to other agents
add-watch.md: code-only changes re-run AST extraction
both speak the Redis protocol on port 6379
hooks.md: run /graphify --update manually for doc/image changes
both are post-commit/push automation that keeps derived artifacts current
query.md: the next --update extracts this Q&A as a node
update.md: changed video files run transcribe.md first
CLAUDE.md: run graphify update . after modifying code
same pnpm test/lint/typecheck gates as the CI pipeline
CONTRIBUTING.md: fill out the PR template completely
CONTRIBUTING.md: ensure main is green before tagging; release.yml deploys
